findings.message: get_stock now reads data["reserved_count"] from the inventory response, but the consumer pact in tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py (with_body declares only item_id and in_stock) does not declare reserved_count. The client depends on a field that is not in the contract: the Pact mock server returns only item_id/in_stock, so this call raises KeyError against the mock, and the provider verification never proves it returns reserved_count. The consumer contract was not extended to cover the new field the client now requires.
findings.suggestion: Add reserved_count to the consumer pact body in test_consumer_inventory.py (e.g. .with_body({"item_id": match.int(1), "in_stock": match.int(5), "reserved_count": match.int(0)})), update the assertion to the new dict return shape, and regenerate/publish the pact so the provider verifies the field before the client relies on it.
